steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice most effectively uses data from the graph. Only claims you can verify directly from the bar heights count. The passage is limited to organizations filing Form 990-PF.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — references Form 990 filers: Wrong. The graph covers only Form 990-PF filers; this choice goes out of scope. ✗
  • Choice B — claims an average: Wrong. The graph shows percentages, not averages you can calculate. ✗
  • Choice C — misstates the self-dealing trend: Wrong. The bar heights don't support this claim. ✗
  • Choice D — undistributed income penalties were a larger share in 2003 than in 2005: Correct. The graph shows 55% in 2003 vs. 40% in 2005 for undistributed income — directly readable from the bars. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
